text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Whispering Oaks, there lived an unusual cat. This wasn’t your run-of-the-month kind of kitty. Nope. This cat had whiskers big enough to catch insects. Its name was Odd.

Odd had an extraordinary talent—if you called it talent—at discovering hidden things. One morning, while lazily stalking the pigeons through the oak trees, Odd drooled at a patch of moss he’d never noticed before.

That evening, Odd discovered an ancient-looking book tucked in-between some branches. It was filled with strange symbols. Curious, Odd flipped through the pages. Every little symbol made Odd twitchier.

By moonlight, he deciphered the strange language. Someone hadn’t just forgotten their book; they’d been studying owl behavior. Odd realized those symbols were guides for feeding wild owls.

That autumn, Odd fed many owls. His secret talent blossomed into a hobby.Neighbors admired Odd. He became Whispering Oaks’ most
